name: Archive Downloader
description: Download files from archive.org items directly to your SD card over WiFi. Native UI rewrite.
author: Corey

changelog.v2.3.0: Multi-select now has its own footer: normal mode shows A Download / X Filters / Y Search / Select Multi / B Back; pressing Select switches to a clean multi-select footer (A Select / L1-R1 All-None / Start Download / Select Exit / B Back). Filters and search are locked out inside multi-select so ticks can't be lost by the list changing underneath. Fixed the keeper picker so Select can no longer disable its checkboxes.
changelog.v2.2.0: Keeper picker: when one archive extracts multiple different files (e.g. several versions of a game), a list appears to tick which to KEEP - the rest are removed. Safe by design: B or confirming nothing keeps everything, cue/m3u/gdi playlists keep their referenced tracks automatically, same-name multi-part games (cue+bin) never prompt, and only files from that extraction can be touched.
changelog.v2.1.0: Filters are OFF by default - config filters now appear in the filter menu as toggles instead of silently trimming the list. X button opens filters, Y opens search directly. Multi-select actually enabled (Select toggles, Start downloads the queue, L1/R1 select/deselect all). Partial files removed when a download is cancelled.
changelog.v2.0.0: Complete rewrite in Go using the gabagool UI library (same UI stack as Pak Store): native list UI with automatic scrolling for long filenames, multi-select download queue with per-file progress and speed, no more screen flashing, built-in zip/7z/rar extraction, single binary replaces curl/jq/yq/7zz/minui tools. All v1.3 features ported: config.yml platforms, per-platform filters and extraction rules, on-device filter menu, search, cached file lists, favorites, arcade name mapping, archive.org login.
